items.correct: The absolute oneness and indivisibility of Allah — the most fundamental Islamic belief. Its opposite, shirk (associating partners with God), is the gravest sin.
items.misconception: Tawhid is the Islamic word for the Trinity.
items.random_wrong: Tawhid is the name of an Islamic prayer position.
items.why: Tawhid is the strict denial of any plurality in God — directly contrasting with Trinitarian Christianity. Students sometimes invert the meaning entirely. The Quran's emphasis on Tawhid shapes Islamic theology, art (no images) and ethics.

items.q: What is the difference between Sunni and Shia Islam?
items.correct: They split over who should lead the Muslim community after Muhammad: Sunnis accepted Abu Bakr and elected caliphs; Shias believed leadership should follow Muhammad's bloodline through Ali.
items.misconception: Sunnis and Shias believe in different gods.
items.random_wrong: Sunnis follow the Quran and Shias follow the Bible.
items.why: Both share the Quran, prophet and core beliefs. The original split was political — about succession — and theological differences developed later. Students inflate it into a different religion.

items.correct: The Islamic declaration of faith: 'There is no god but Allah, and Muhammad is the messenger of Allah' — affirming monotheism and Muhammad's prophethood.
items.misconception: The Shahada is the requirement to pray five times a day.
items.random_wrong: The Shahada is the Islamic pilgrimage to Mecca.
items.why: Students confuse the five pillars. Shahada is the declaration, Salah is prayer, Hajj is pilgrimage — each is a distinct pillar with a different function.
Correct answer: The oneness of Allah. “The five pillars” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which is NOT one of the Six Articles of Sunni Islam?
items.why: Correct answer: Imamah. “Angels” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many Articles of Faith are in Sunni Islam?
items.why: Exact answer: 6. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
